content: Phase III trials are large-scale, definitive studies designed to demonstrate drug efficacy and safety for regulatory approval. These pivotal trials typically enroll 300-3,000+ patients across multiple centers and countries, lasting 1-4 years. Phase III confirms the benefit-risk profile established in Phase II and provides the primary evidence for FDA/EMA approval. Trials can be superiority (demonstrating better efficacy than control) or non-inferiority (showing not unacceptably worse than active comparator). Approximately 50-60% of drugs entering Phase III successfully gain approval.
